A red indicator with a black dot in the middle signals fully applied brakes. In this case the parking brake is applied. If the black dot is not fully centered the brakes are only partially applied.

In simplified terms: the brake mode modifies the application speed of brakes so they apply more evenly and smoothly through long trains but still as rapidly as safely possible. This innovation from the 1910s avoids excessive forces on the couplers and cars running into each other.
